changes.before: Jaina lay on the narrow cot with her palm braced against the flagstones at the head of it and counted the gaps Frostmourne kicked down from the evidence vault above.
changes.after: Jaina lay on the narrow cot, palm braced against the flagstones, and counted the gaps the evidence vault above kicked down into her cell.

changes.before: The pulse moved through the palace like hostile rhythm, no longer broad and dumb. It had narrowed since the verdict. It was reaching.
changes.after: The pulse had narrowed since the verdict. It was reaching.

changes.before: Worse than that, each pulse came back carrying traces. She could not read names through two floors of stone and an evidence room built for ordinary crimes.
changes.after: Worse, each pulse came back carrying traces.

changes.before: She stopped before she turned it into numbers, projections, and all the other things that belonged to a Kirin Tor worktable instead of a prison wall.
What she wanted to say had been waiting since the throne room. Since before that, if she was honest. Since Northrend.
changes.after: What she wanted to say had been waiting since the throne room. Since Northrend, if she was honest.

changes.before: Kel'Thuzad's hand had been careful on the page, margins neat enough to make the horror look orderly. And he had done it alone. Arthas had gone the same way by a different road.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad had gone alone. Arthas too, by a different road.

changes.before: "And one answer would have gotten me killed," Jaina drew a breath and kept going before shame could close her throat.
changes.after: "And one answer would have gotten me killed." Jaina kept going before shame could close her throat.

changes.before: "We stopped him because we were right together, with both of us doing the work."
changes.after: "We stopped him because we were right together."

changes.before: The cot slat under her thigh gave one dry tick. Fine hairs lifted along her forearm. The pulse had leaned toward the place her words had landed.
changes.after: The pulse had leaned toward the place her words had landed.

changes.before: The blade's cold she knew now. It came from above and soaked downward through the vault floor and the palace foundations, steady as seep-water. This was different.
changes.after: The blade's cold seeped down from above. This gathered under the door and stood there.

changes.before: The thing had presence enough to make the cot, bucket, and barred door feel like toys arranged around it.
changes.after: The next it held another body, crowding the room by sheer size.

changes.before: "Arthas had done what was needed. After that, the question was yield."
changes.after: "Arthas had done what was needed. After that, the question was how much it bought."

changes.before: "The anti-blade spell surprised me," the creature said after a beat.
changes.after: "The spell that checked the blade surprised me," the creature said.

changes.before: The old grain trader near Millhaven... The trader east of Andorhal... The figure near the Barov trees... The aide in Lor'themar's camp... The merchant in Stratholme...
changes.after: Millhaven. Andorhal. The Barov trees. Lor'themar's tent. Stratholme. Five faces. One farewell.

changes.before: Not the whole thing at once. Too much, too raw, too easy to lose in the wall-code they used. Just the hook of it.
changes.after: She would have to give Jaina the hook when the corridor quieted.

observations: Wordcount pass cut 1201 words from the chapter without removing any plot turn.
The opening countdown had multiple redundant restatements of the same pressure signal; those were collapsed.
Cell description lost one sour-water sentence layer while keeping dungeon texture.
The pulse description repeated its hostile reach twice; reduced to one clean threat beat.
Guard-mind sampling kept the useful trace and dropped the legal-architecture aside.
Jaina's prison-wall report now stays in scene instead of unpacking itself for the reader.
The 'numbers, projections' sentence read like commentary on prose register; cut to a single in-voice clause.
Jaina's confession ladder had three separate 'since' beats; reduced to two.
Kel'Thuzad and Arthas mirror paragraph was doing interpretation the dialogue already carried.
The Millhaven-Andorhal-Scholomance-Northrend list still lands, but the ownership gloss is tighter.
Jaina's key admission now ends on 'right together' instead of a redundant work-summary tag.
The truth-trigger pulse lost extra cot/hair/finger reactions and now snaps straight to consequence.
Part I exit keeps the wall-code image while removing one repeated chain paragraph.
Second-cold entrance cut repeated explanation of how ordinary darkness differs from this one.
Visitor entrance lost toy-room rhetoric and kept the size beat that matters.
Sylvanas still reads the room tactically; the trimmed version sharpens that POV instead of softening it.
The creature's dialogue had one document-register leak in 'yield'; replaced with a more lived construction.
The creature's dialogue had one meta-register leak in 'anti-blade spell'; replaced with grounded phrasing.
The visitor still sounds old and controlled after the cuts; no loss of menace register.
Recognition sequence no longer spends a full sentence per disguise before making the point.
The farewell montage now lands as pattern-recognition rather than recap.
The phrase-to-Jaina relay is cleaner and more tactical, which suits Sylvanas.
Simile load dropped from like 4 to 1.
As-if constructions dropped from 3 to 1.
Vague 'something' dropped from 1 to 0.
